mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid improvement in labor market conditions signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns as investors price in higher earnings multiples.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising consumer credit and increasing housing starts indicates synchronized expansion in durable goods spending and construction activity, which amplifies positive earnings surprises and lifts equity prices.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS growth × HOUST growth) shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward SPX returns over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of explicit inflation targeting, deviations of core consumer prices from the central bank's implicit 2% target force policy normalization, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL rises above the 2% annualized target while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real gross domestic product expands the corporate earnings base, directly supporting higher equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 across all available data windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in industrial production capacity utilization signals supply-side bottlenecks easing, which improves corporate margins and drives positive equity returns as operational leverage increases.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of observations following a 3-month INDPRO acceleration exceeding 2.0% over a 15-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment gains and stagnant wage growth indicates slack in the labor market, which suppresses inflation expectations and allows equities to rally on growth-without-inflation dynamics.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in at least 65% of historical instances over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid contraction in consumer credit availability forces a pullback in discretionary spending, directly reducing corporate revenues and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically neutral when RSAFS contracts by more than 5.0% over a two-month window across a 5-year backtest.
